name: Saceruela y Los Pozuelos de Calatrava desde Piedrabuena – circular por Luciana y Tabla de la Yedra
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Piedrabuena traverse a diverse landscape at the entrance to the Montes de Toledo, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The region features a blend of gently undulating areas, shaped by ancient lava flows, and more challenging massifs and sierras with moderate to steep slopes. The Bullaque River flows through the area, creating a rich environment, and the landscape also bears marks of a volcanic past. This topography provides road cycling routes of varying difficulty, suitable for different fitness levels. Best road cycling routes around Piedrabuena The most popular road cycling route is Abenójar, Saceruela, and Luciana Loop, a 72.0 miles (116.0 km) trail that takes 5 hours 22 minutes to complete. This difficult route features over 3,336 feet (1017 m) of elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Circular by Piedrabuena and the Tabla de la Yedra (Río Bullaque) — from Porzuna, a moderate 32.8 miles (52.8 km) path. This route offers views of the Bullaque River and the natural enclave of Tabla de la Yedra. Local road cyclists also love the By the Pozuelos de Calatrava and the Tabla de la Yedra — circular by Piedrabuena, a 65.0 miles (104.7 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 34 minutes. Road cycling around Piedrabuena is defined by the Montes de Toledo, the Bullaque River, and volcanic terrain, offering a mix of undulating areas and challenging sierras. The network provides options for different ability levels, from relaxed rides to demanding ascents. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 90 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Piedrabuena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Piedrabuena?
A: Piedrabuena offers a diverse landscape for road cyclists. You'll find a mix of gently undulating areas, shaped by ancient lava flows, and more challenging massifs and sierras with moderate to steep slopes, especially as you approach the Montes de Toledo. This variety provides routes suitable for different fitness levels, from relaxed rides to demanding ascents.

Q: Are there routes suitable for experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge?
A: Yes, Piedrabuena is an excellent destination for experienced cyclists. The region, particularly at the entrance to the Montes de Toledo, features challenging massifs and sierras with significant elevation gains. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251040932/abenojar-saceruela-and-luciana-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abenójar, Saceruela, and Luciana Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 72-mile route with over 3,336 feet (1017 m) of elevation gain. Another demanding option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086215201/saceruela-y-los-pozuelos-de-calatrava-desde-piedrabuena-circular-por-luciana-y-tabla-de-la-yedra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saceruela y Los Pozuelos de Calatrava desde Piedrabuena&lt;/a&gt;, covering over 81 miles with more than 3,600 feet (1100 m) of climbing.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while cycling in Piedrabuena?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle alongside the Bullaque River, which creates a vibrant environment, and visit the stunning "Tabla de la Yedra" with its lush poplars and pools. The landscape also bears marks of a volcanic past, offering unique geological interest. Additionally, routes around the Vicario Reservoir provide peaceful and visually rewarding experiences.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular moderate option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1366186240/circular-by-piedrabuena-and-the-tabla-de-la-yedra-rio-bullaque-from-porzuna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular by Piedrabuena and the Tabla de la Yedra (Río Bullaque) — from Porzuna&lt;/a&gt;, which is 32.8 miles long. For a longer, more challenging circular ride, consider &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251026703/by-the-pozuelos-de-calatrava-and-the-tabla-de-la-yedra-circular-by-piedrabuena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;By the Pozuelos de Calatrava and the Tabla de la Yedra — circular by Piedrabuena&lt;/a&gt;, a 65-mile route.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I discover along the routes?
A: Beyond natural beauty, Piedrabuena offers cultural and historical points of interest. You might encounter the historic Sheep Bridge (Puente de las Ovejas/Merinas), once used for sheep migration, or the 19th-century Martinete iron foundry. The remains of the Mortara Castle and the impressive Parish Church also provide cultural enrichment. For specific viewpoints, consider highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5645257" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedrabuena Antennas&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5776166" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Cruz De Mayo (Porzuna)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Piedrabuena?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling in Piedrabuena, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be quite hot, especially in July and August, while winters are typically cooler but still offer rideable days. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes?
A: While many routes in Piedrabuena feature moderate to difficult terrain due to the Montes de Toledo, there are options that are less demanding. The region's gently undulating areas, particularly those shaped by ancient lava flows, can offer more relaxed rides. Look for routes categorized as 'moderate' on komoot, which will have less extreme elevation changes compared to the 'difficult' routes.

Q: Where can I find parking facilities for my car when starting a road cycling route?
A: Piedrabuena, as a local municipality, typically offers various public parking areas within the town. When planning your route, it's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen komoot tour for specific parking recommendations or look for general parking in the town center, which usually provides convenient access to the surrounding routes.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Piedrabuena?
A: Road cyclists often praise Piedrabuena for its diverse and picturesque landscapes, which offer a unique blend of volcanic terrain, riverine environments, and the challenging beauty of the Montes de Toledo. The variety of routes, from scenic riverside paths to demanding mountain climbs, is frequently highlighted, providing options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The "Tabla de la Yedra" along the Bullaque River is a particularly stunning natural enclave, perfect for a scenic break. Routes around the Vicario Reservoir also offer expansive water views. For elevated perspectives, consider routes that pass near highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2718219" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Picón&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6264366" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Almagrero&lt;/a&gt;, which are mountain peaks in the area.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay overnight in Piedrabuena?
A: Yes, Piedrabuena offers amenities for visitors. You can find local cafes and pubs in the town for refreshments and meals. For overnight stays, there are various accommodation options, including hotels, guesthouses, and rural accommodation, providing a comfortable base for exploring the region by bike.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, Piedrabuena is a great starting point for longer rides. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086215201/saceruela-y-los-pozuelos-de-calatrava-desde-piedrabuena-circular-por-luciana-y-tabla-de-la-yedra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saceruela y Los Pozuelos de Calatrava desde Piedrabuena&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, covers over 81 miles (131 km), making it suitable for cyclists looking for an extended challenge.
tips: Located in the Maiyor square next to the Town Hall. It is a Neoclassical style construction. Among the construction materials, brick predominates, and of course volcanic stone, and the metallic roofs of the towers stand out. Very nice in general everything.

It is an impressive iron foundry built around the 1940s and 1950s in the historic region of Campo de Calatrava, next to the Guadiana River, 4.6 km away. north of the municipality of Los Pozuelos de Calatrava. Work sheds, a huge oven, press, canal, and other elements are preserved in different states of ruin.

Located in the central part of the province, the surroundings of Los Pozuelos de Calatrava are characterized by presenting a soft relief characteristic of the Campo de Calatrava region. It is crossed from east to west by the Guadiana River and streams such as Fresno.
Regarding its heritage, the Church of Our Lady of the Visitation stands out.

The town of is one of the oldest towns in the area, at the service of pilgrims on the road to Andalusia. Today it sits on the C-424 highway that connects Puertollano with Almadén. You can stop to rest in the tree-lined Plaza de San Juan Bautista and admire the façade of the parish church, built with local stone and baked red brick.

Saceruela is a small rural town next to the CM-415 road. Rest in the Nuestra Señora de las Cruces Parish located in the Plaza de la Constitución. The town is one of the starting points of the historic route of the Camino de Guadalupe de Levante that connects the Iberian Peninsula with the town of Guadalupe in Cáceres.

In the western part of the province and the historic Campo de Calatrava, between the Canalizos and San Antón mountain ranges and the Guadiana valley, with very rugged relief and evocative landscapes, is the pleasant town of Luciana.

It houses the mouths of two tributaries of the Guadiana River: the Bullaque in the northeast, very close to the town, and the Tirteafuera in the southwest, in Tablacaldera. Water, always very present, gives uniqueness and beauty to the environment.

As it passes through Piedrabuena, the Bullaque River has an area of calm waters known as the Tabla de la Yedra. This is a place of recreation that is visited annually by thousands of people, being one of the most outstanding spaces equipped for bathing in the province of Ciudad Real.
